author: @AtrumOculis
text: Very nice mechanism indeed.  The child on the swing does move faster when pushed harder, but the frequency stays the same.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: to be honest mate I think that depends on what you mean by faster - the frequency - which is the time between swings is constant then no they don't move faster - but if you mean distance traveled - which is a change in amplitude and therefore an increase in a distance traveled in a fixed time then yes the speed of travel increases.

replies.author: @AtrumOculis
replies.text: Hello Robert, my mistake, i assummed you meant the childs velocity but you meant frequency.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: @AtrumOculis It's all right mate - sometimes I can be a bit unclear mate - so thank you for pointing it out

author: @mosesarambula2090
text: Is there away together a hold of you? Like an email?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: You can always post your question here mate - i will try to answer

replies.author: @mosesarambula2090
replies.text:
​@ThinkingandTinkering Well... Discamer: I understand that perpetual motors are fake. That being said, I've been designing a EPC (Electric Pedal Car) that doesn't need a charge. I am a blind Carpenter from California and have no background in Electric or Mechanical engineering, other than books. Would you be willing to work with me? I have been using your channel to prove my ideas. Would you help me? 

   Thank you for reading

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: @mosesarambula2090 Hiya mate - I am sorry but i don't get involved in other folks projects - I am not saying this to be a jerk - it's just I get 3-400 hundred emails a day and in there will be 10-20 requests just like yours - I spend about 2 hours a day replying to emails, comments and posts and then I just have to stop or I would have no time for my own projects. So, there just isn't the time I am afraid to help folks on an individual basis - the best I can do is videos - again - I am sorry but I do wish you the best

replies.author: @mosesarambula2090
replies.text: ​@ThinkingandTinkeringNo, I figured. But I had to ask, for peace of mind. I've been doing this  for like nine years. Thank you for getting back to me, I appreciate that the most.

author: @kadmow
text:
I was thinking - many of the the mechanical marvels we have created are really only still produced as fascinating "complications" in a world of MEMS and electronics...  

(true, mechanics can be "maintained" beyond a natural working life, but not without a fair bit of skill and/or access to some technology - electronics and MEMS devices less so able to be "jury rigged" into life again - by many..)

author: @chris993361
text: You can actually take those cheaper bearings, rip the seals out, use some solvent to get rid of the original grease, replace it with a real light oil. Leave the seals off and you will get a real low friction bearing. For a lot of uses you don't really need the seals.

replies.author: @kadmow
replies.text:
- for lube - under "no load" - some graphite powder - doesn't attract additional dust.
 yes "no shields" give lowest friction.. - reasons why hard gems and point pivots are used in mechanical clocks and watches..

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Good tip!

author: @charlesjmouse
text:
Always interesting, thank you.
The tourbillion - I suspect the primary reason why there are those who swear by it and those who swear at it is the subject is an almost endless rabbit hole of application and situation. Just one example: I would suggest it's worth has a deal to do with the mass of the moving components. The 'smaller' the less benefit such a device is likely to confer... just as well as the harder it becomes to construct.

replies.author: @IsZomg
replies.text: The tourbillion definitely helped accuracy back in the days of pocket watches because a pocket watch sits in your pocket the whole day long in the same orientation. They are much less useful for wristwatches because a wristwatch is always moving and pointing in different directions throughout the day. Only the very best of high end wristwatches actually gain accuracy with the inclusion of tourbillions (see Greubel Forsey using two tourbillions at different angles to win the International Chronometry Competition in 2011). Most tourbillion watches just use them because they look amazing.

replies.author: @kadmow
replies.text: @IsZomg  - unique complications sell - high end, unique watches...

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I suspect you are right mate

replies.author: @selfcorrected-Bobby
replies.text: Magnets are also stores of energy and I will die (might have to ) saying that maglev magnets from space will save Earth! :p  No friction there except atmosphere and gravity to reduce the output endurance of the magnet...? if the maglev rotor's topology compensates not only for Earnhaw's theorem but precipitates and sustains spin also, concurrently not equiring a bearing as its 'bearing' (like that of a ship)  is defined by its topology and is not an additional component of the design, id est the common friction-based 'bearing'... i mean what about a "one way oscillator - wouldn't be an oscillator would it?" it regains more energy and piggy backs. The videos are interesting because they present ideological properties and not only the limitations of the instantiated example, bringing about a large perspicability of review. The topology of non-fallacious  knowledge-transmissability to other concepts is remarkably interesting, from a linguistically semantic perspective. I'm still concerned that I cannot afford any experiments though. One day I will, but it would have been a waste of time to have waited so long, knowing already how right I am... Extemporaneity is not amusing! - balance wheel and spring would be one and the same in the design of a maglev rotor. - not sure if I'm makgin sence (no offensce) but I'm saying this because I would not have orded it in this manner. It is interesting to see a true genius conveying ideas and also instantiations, the problem is people cannot topologically reapply the ideas you convey to other constructs. What is conveyed here is remarkably sound and it's apparent people aren't capable of retopologicalizationifying of ideology to other application constructs - forget pontificating.
